value.body:
## Context

The app shell/navigation sidebar needs a collapsible interaction so users can reclaim horizontal space without losing access to navigation.

## Outcome

Introduce an accessible collapsed and expanded sidebar experience with a visible UI toggle, keyboard support, appropriate preference persistence, and clean layout behavior in both states.

## Scope

- Add a visible control to collapse and expand the app shell/navigation sidebar.
- Add a documented keyboard shortcut for toggling the sidebar.
- Review the existing shortcut registry before selecting the shortcut to avoid conflicts.
- Persist the collapsed state when consistent with the app's existing preference behavior.
- Keep navigation items understandable and operable in the collapsed state, including accessible names, focus behavior, tooltips or equivalent context where needed, and keyboard navigation.
- Ensure the main content layout adapts cleanly when the sidebar changes width.
- Update relevant UI tests and interaction coverage for the toggle, shortcut, persistence behavior, and both layouts.

## Acceptance criteria

- Users can collapse and expand the sidebar using a visible UI control.
- Users can toggle the sidebar with a documented keyboard shortcut.
- The shortcut does not conflict with existing application shortcuts.
- Sidebar state behaves consistently across navigation and, where supported by existing app preferences, reloads.
- Navigation remains accessible and usable in both collapsed and expanded states.
- The layout adapts cleanly in both states without overlap, clipping, or unexpected content shifts.
- Relevant UI tests or interaction coverage verify the toggle, shortcut, persistence, and accessible collapsed/expanded behavior.

## Risks

- Shortcut conflicts or platform-specific keyboard conventions.
- Persisted state becoming inconsistent with responsive or narrow-window behavior.
- Icon-only collapsed navigation losing accessible context.
value.title: Add collapsible app navigation sidebar
